cost_sensitivity.cost_model: Future diagnostics must reject interpretation if any open-or-close window readout only survives before fees, slippage, and spread assumptions.

cost_assumptions.scope: research planning only; no execution or tradability claim

data_assumptions.data_dependency_detail: Fully covered by the existing minutes_from_rth_open / minutes_to_rth_close / rth_segment_flag session features; no new FeatureRequest and no external feed required for the clock-position conditioner. A literal auction-imbalance feed (MOC/LOC imbalance, opening imbalance) is paid data and is explicitly OUT OF SCOPE / deferred for this card.
data_assumptions.missing_feature_candidates: No new feature candidates are declared by this legacy card.
data_assumptions.missing_label_candidates: No new label candidates are declared by this legacy card.

exclusion_rules: Define the open/close proximity windows as thresholds on the existing minutes_from_rth_open / minutes_to_rth_close features (which already account for the actual session window via the session template and SessionWindowState classification), NOT as fixed wall-clock times, so EARLY_CLOSE sessions are handled correctly. No new feature and no metadata surface is required; the windows are pure thresholds on existing point-in-time clocks.
Do not test needs_paid_data records in V0; paid-data sourcing is RED-deferred.
Do not edit or delete the legacy Track-A card until a later retirement phase is authorized.
expected_failure_modes: Conditioning on a known-ahead session-clock position is structurally distinct from the realized-price/quote main effects the dead mechanisms conditioned on. The MAIN orthogonality risk here is that vwap_session (a dead mechanism) is itself session-anchored, so an into-the-close effect could be adjacent to vwap_session dynamics; orthogonality must be demonstrated, not assumed. This is the weakest-orthogonality intraday card.

Residualize forward outcomes against the vwap_session and regime dead-mechanism factors before scoring the open/close-proximity conditioner, and report the conditioner's marginal contribution on the residual. Confirm the open and close windows are tested as one pre-registered conditioner (two thresholds), not split into two separate hypotheses, to avoid inflating the surface.

Fully covered by the existing minutes_from_rth_open / minutes_to_rth_close / rth_segment_flag session features; no new FeatureRequest and no external feed required for the clock-position conditioner. A literal auction-imbalance feed (MOC/LOC imbalance, opening imbalance) is paid data and is explicitly OUT OF SCOPE / deferred for this card.
promotion_criteria: Any future WATCH or CANDIDATE routing requires reviewer-gated evidence and must preserve EXPLORATORY promotion guards.
Any future diagnostic must pass no-lookahead, duplicate-exposure, and testability checks before interpretation.

timestamp_assumptions.lookahead_description: The open/close proximity windows are derived entirely from the existing point-in-time minutes_from_rth_open / minutes_to_rth_close clocks, which are known-ahead given the published session template. The hazard is using a fixed wall-clock cutoff that is wrong on EARLY_CLOSE sessions, where the close shifts earlier.
timestamp_assumptions.lookahead_level: low lookahead risk declared by the legacy Track-A card

hypothesis_card.author: ALPHA_IDEA_TO_VERDICT_LOOP_V0/IVL-P01
hypothesis_card.expected_mechanism: We test whether intraday forward behavior conditioned on the known-ahead intraday session-position windows around the RTH open and the RTH close (the first/last minutes of the regular session, where auction-adjacent flow concentrates) differs from the mid-session. This is a falsifiable intraday-seasonality conditioning hypothesis, not a profitability claim.
falsification_criteria: Reject or requeue this migrated record if required governed features or labels are unavailable before diagnostics.
Block any later verdict if no-lookahead controls or duplicate exposure checks fail before a metric is inspected.
hypothesis_card.rationale: Auction-adjacent flow (opening price discovery, closing-auction imbalance into MOC/LOC orders) concentrates at deterministic, known-ahead points of the regular session clock; whether the minutes adjacent to those windows carry a measurable conditional forward pattern is an empirical question the diagnostics resolve. The closing-auction itself is not directly observed without paid imbalance data; the conditioner here is calendar/clock position alone.
risks: The open/close proximity windows are derived entirely from the existing point-in-time minutes_from_rth_open / minutes_to_rth_close clocks, which are known-ahead given the published session template. The hazard is using a fixed wall-clock cutoff that is wrong on EARLY_CLOSE sessions, where the close shifts earlier.

Conditioning on a known-ahead session-clock position is structurally distinct from the realized-price/quote main effects the dead mechanisms conditioned on. The MAIN orthogonality risk here is that vwap_session (a dead mechanism) is itself session-anchored, so an into-the-close effect could be adjacent to vwap_session dynamics; orthogonality must be demonstrated, not assumed. This is the weakest-orthogonality intraday card.
